title: The origins and development of aesthetics.

publication.journal: Philos Trans R Soc Lond B Biol Sci

assets.abstract: All people (and some other animals) have aesthetic responses to sensory stimulation, responses of emotional pleasure or displeasure. These emotions vary from one person and culture to another, yet they share a common mechanism. To survive, an adaptive animal (as opposed to a tropic animal) needs to become comfortable with normality and to have slight abnormalities draw attention to themselves.
